data.title: Why Is ImmunityBio Stock Gaining Tuesday?
data.description:
ImmunityBio Inc. (NASDAQ:IBRX) shares are up during Tuesday’s premarket session as the company is reportedly advancing its regulatory efforts.

This positive movement follows the announcement of productive discussions with the Saudi Food and Drug Authority regarding the submission of a regulatory package for recombinant BCG, aimed at addressing the global BCG shortage.

During these discussions, the Saudi FDA encouraged ImmunityBio to submit a regulatory package for its recombinant BCG (rBCG) to improve access in Saudi Arabia. The company plans to submit this package in the coming weeks, which could significantly impact bladder cancer treatment availability in the region.

Additionally, ImmunityBio is also exploring the expansion of its product Anktiva (nogapendekin alfa inbakicept) in combination with checkpoint inhibitors (CPI) for various tumor types.

The scientific rationale for expanding into additional tumor types in the CPI relapsed population is supported by data from the QUILT-3.055 Phase 2b basket trial (N=147), which enrolled patients across multiple tumor types.

In the NSCLC cohort (N=86), the addition of Anktiva to the same checkpoint inhibitor on which patients had progressed demonstrated a median overall survival of 14.1 months, with 12-month and 18-month survival rates of 57% and 34%, respectively.

ImmunityBio announced preliminary net product revenue for Anktiva reached approximately $113 million for fiscal 2025, reflecting a remarkable 700% increase year-over-year.

Technical Analysis

The stock is currently trading 5.47% above its 20-day simple moving average (SMA) and 12.3% above its 100-day SMA, indicating a strong short-term trend. Shares have increased 77.46% over the past 12 months and are currently positioned closer to their 52-week highs than lows, reflecting robust long-term performance.

The RSI is at 50, indicating a neutral position, while MACD is above its signal line, suggesting bullish momentum. The combination of neutral RSI and bullish MACD indicates mixed momentum, suggesting potential for upward movement.

  • Key Resistance: $8.00
  • Key Support: $5.50

Earnings & Analyst Outlook

The countdown is on: ImmunityBio is set to report earnings on March 2, 2026.

  • EPS Estimate: Loss of 9 cents (Up from Loss of 9 cents)
  • Revenue Estimate: $37.51 million (Up from $7.55 million)

Analyst Consensus & Recent Actions: The stock carries a Buy Rating with an average price target of $11.80. Recent analyst moves include:

  • HC Wainwright & Co.: Buy (Raises Target to $10)
  • D. Boral Capital: Buy (Maintains Target to $24)
  • BTIG: Buy (Raises Target to $9)

Benzinga Edge Rankings

Below is the Benzinga Edge scorecard for ImmunityBio, highlighting its strengths and weaknesses compared to the broader market:

  • Momentum: Bullish (Score: 95.69) — Stock is outperforming the broader market.

Top ETF Exposure

  • Virtus LifeSci Biotech Products ETF (NYSE:BBP): 4.03% Weight

IBRX Price Action: ImmunityBio shares were up 1.51% at $6.040 at the time of publication on Tuesday, according to Benzinga Pro data.

data.title: Palo Alto, Opendoor, Carvana And More Stocks With Earnings This Week
data.description:
The fourth-quarter earnings season is winding down, but there are still several reports ahead this week from retail giants, energy leaders and tech companies.

  • CVNA stock is moving. See the chart and price action here. 

Here's a look at the earnings calendar for the week ahead: 

Tuesday, Feb. 17

Before Market Open:

The holiday-shortened week got underway on Tuesday morning with the following companies reporting before the opening bell: 

  • Medtronic Plc (NYSE:MDT)
  • Energy Transfer LP (NYSE:ET)
  • HIVE Digital Technologies Ltd. (NASDAQ:HIVE)

After Market Close:

Investors will be watching Palo Alto Networks after Tuesday's closing bell. Analysts expect the company to report earnings of 94 cents per share on revenue of $2.58 billion, according to estimates from Benzinga Pro. 

The following companies will also report on Tuesday afternoon:

Wednesday, Feb. 18

Before Market Open:

The following companies are set to report before the market open on Wednesday: 

After Market Close:

Online-only car dealer Carvana Co. (NYSE:CVNA) will release its Q4 results after Wednesday's closing bell. 

While the company continues to project strong year-over-year growth in retail units and gross profit per unit, investor focus has shifted toward a recent short-seller report alleging accounting irregularities and the subsequent pressure on its premium valuation.

Analysts expect Carvana to report quarterly earnings of $1.10 per share on revenue of $5.26 billion, according to estimates from Benzinga Pro. 

Thursday, Feb. 19

Before Market Open: 

All eyes will be on Walmart Inc. (NYSE:WMT) on Thursday morning, when the $1 trillion retail giant reports before the opening bell. 

Investors will be closely watching for continued momentum in high-margin segments like advertising and e-commerce, as well as commentary on the health of the consumer. 

Wall Street is looking for quarterly earnings of 72 cents per share on revenue of $190.24 billion, according to estimates from Benzinga Pro. 

The following companies will also report on Thursday morning: 

After Market Close:

Meme stock and iBuyer, Opendoor Technologies Inc. (NASDAQ:OPEN), will report Q4 results after Thursday's closing bell. 

Investors will be looking for updates on its turnaround plans and progress toward 2026 profitability, with a focus on improving gross margins and the success of the company’s AI-driven “capital-light” platform. 

The week wraps up with reports expected from:

data.title: Radware, Bell Cyber Expand Partnership To Fight Rising Cyberattacks
data.description:
Radware Ltd. (NASDAQ:RDWR) on Tuesday announced an expanded partnership with Bell Cyber to enhance AI-driven, cloud-delivered security services. The collaboration is designed to address the growing sophistication of cyber threats as enterprises face increasing digital and cloud-based security challenges.

Unified Web And API Security

The collaboration integrates Radware’s AI-based application security with Bell Cyber’s managed security operations, enabling earlier detection of anomalous behavior and blocking malicious traffic. This service is now available through Bell’s managed services portfolio, providing bilingual support for Canadian customers and extending its reach to U.S.-based organizations.

The offering combines web application protection, API security, and bot mitigation into a single managed service, simplifying security measures for clients. This initiative reflects Radware’s commitment to enhancing its security solutions to address evolving cyber threats.

Recently, Radware announced that its board authorized a new share repurchase program of up to $80 million in ordinary shares under its 2026 Plan, which runs through March 15, 2027.

On the previous trading day, the broader market declined, with the S&P 500 falling 0.30%. Radware’s stock performance contrasts with the broader market trend, suggesting that company-specific developments may be shaping its current trajectory.

Technical Analysis

The stock is currently trading 15.2% below its 100-day simple moving average (SMA), indicating some short-term weakness, while it has increased by 17.26% over the past 12 months. Radware’s shares are closer to their 52-week highs than to their lows, suggesting relatively strong performance in the longer term.

The RSI is at a neutral level, indicating that the stock is neither overbought nor oversold. Meanwhile, MACD is below its signal line, suggesting bearish pressure on the stock.

The combination of neutral RSI and bearish MACD suggests mixed momentum.

  • Key Resistance: $31.50
  • Key Support: $26.00

This expansion of services with Bell Cyber is significant, as it enhances Radware’s offerings in a competitive market, enabling it to better serve clients facing increasingly sophisticated cyber threats. The partnership not only strengthens Radware’s market position but also aligns with its strategy to deliver comprehensive security solutions.

Earnings & Analyst Outlook

Radware is slated to provide its next financial update by May 6, 2026.

  • EPS Estimate: 24 cents (Down from 27 cents YoY)
  • Revenue Estimate: $78.72 Million (Up from $72.08 Million YoY)
  • Valuation: P/E of 62.0x (Indicates premium valuation)

Benzinga Edge Rankings

Below is the Benzinga Edge scorecard for Radware, highlighting its strengths and weaknesses compared to the broader market:

  • Value: Weak (Score: 31.77) — Trading at a steep premium relative to peers.
  • Quality: Strong (Score: 74.61) — Balance sheet remains healthy.
  • Momentum: Neutral (Score: 66.84) — Stock is performing in line with the broader market.

The Verdict: Radware’s Benzinga Edge signal reveals a mixed outlook. While the Quality score indicates a healthy balance sheet, the Value score suggests that the stock may be overvalued relative to its peers.

RDWR Price Action: Radware shares closed 5.80% higher at $27.92 on Friday, according to Benzinga Pro data.
